videos.title: 3D Shapes I Know | Faces, Edges and Vertices | Solid Shapes Song
videos.description: Learn about the faces, edges, and vertices of 3D shapes through a fun song! Discover how many flat faces a cube has, how many edges a pyramid has, and how to count the corners (vertices) of different solid shapes.
learningObjectives: Understand what faces, edges, and vertices are
Begin counting faces, edges, and vertices
keyConceptsCovered: Face = flat surface of a 3D shape
Edge = line where two faces meet
Vertex = corner point where edges meet
followUpTopics: Counting faces, edges, vertices of each shape

videos.title: Counting Faces, Edges, and Vertices of 3D Shapes
videos.description: Learn to count the faces, edges, and vertices of cubes, rectangular prisms, pyramids, cylinders, and cones step by step. See clear diagrams for each shape.

videos.title: Shape Properties Table | Organizing Faces, Edges, Vertices
videos.description: Learn to organize the number of faces, edges, and vertices of different 3D shapes in a neat table. Compare the properties of cubes, pyramids, prisms, and more!
learningObjectives: Create a properties table for 3D shapes
keyConceptsCovered: Organizing shape data in a table

videos.title: Faces, Edges, and Vertices Quiz | Test Yourself!
videos.description: Can you count the faces, edges, and vertices of different 3D shapes? Take this fun quiz and check your understanding!

videos.title: Building 3D Shapes | Nets and Folding
videos.description: See how 3D shapes are made from flat 2D nets! Watch a cube net fold into a cube, count the faces on the net, and understand how edges and vertices come together.
learningObjectives: Understand how nets fold into 3D shapes
keyConceptsCovered: Folding a net creates edges where faces meet
Vertices form where edges come together

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Fast-track through faces, edges, vertices with building challenge

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of faces, edges, and vertices with practice and nets

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Core faces, edges, vertices concepts after prerequisite review

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites
after_prerequisite_review.rationale: Essential faces, edges, vertices content only

questions.question: How many faces does a cube have?
questions.explanation: A cube has 6 faces. Think of a dice - each number is on a different face, and there are 6 numbers (1 to 6), so 6 faces!

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Use physical 3D shape models and let students count faces, edges, and vertices by touching them. Build shapes from nets (paper cutouts) to make it hands-on.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Help your child count the faces of a box, the edges of a tissue box, and the corners of a dice. Everyday objects make great learning tools!
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Pick up a box or a dice and try counting the flat faces, the edges where two faces meet, and the pointy corners (vertices). Write the numbers in a table!
